source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, Government of India

guidelines.notes: Critical to give within 24 hours for maximum protection against perinatal transmission

guidelines.contraindications: Anaphylaxis to previous dose, encephalopathy within 7 days of previous DTP
guidelines.notes: Three doses required. Common side effects: fever, pain at injection site. Give paracetamol if fever.
Do not feed child for 30 minutes after dose. If child vomits within 10 minutes, repeat dose.

guidelines.contraindications: Pregnancy (in mothers), severe immunodeficiency, anaphylaxis to egg protein
guidelines.notes: Two doses required. Mild fever and rash possible 5-12 days after vaccination — normal.

guidelines.contraindications: Severe combined immunodeficiency, history of intussusception
guidelines.notes: Protects against severe diarrhea. Should be given before 24 weeks of age for first dose.
Protects against pneumonia and meningitis. Three-dose schedule.

guidelines.age: Starting 9 months, then every 6 months until 5 years
guidelines.dose: 100,000 IU at 9 months; 200,000 IU from 18 months
guidelines.notes: Critical for preventing blindness and boosting immunity. Give with MR vaccine at 9 months.

asha_responsibilities: Maintain child immunization register for all children under 5 in assigned area
Remind parents 2-3 days before due vaccination date
Accompany mother and child to immunization session
Record vaccination in mother-child protection (MCP) card
Report any adverse events following immunization (AEFI) to ANM/PHC
Identify children who have missed doses and ensure catch-up vaccination
